narrative: OMX30 opens in solid bull market positioning with +9.8% above the 200-day moving average and MACD above signal, though technicals show overbought conditions (Stochastic K=81.4, RSI 61.42 approaching 70). Copper's +0.69% rally is a tailwind for industrials and Boliden. Declining US 10Y yields (-0.61% to 4.558%) ease refinancing concerns for real estate and reduce DCF compression risk for tech. However, stochastic overbought and 1.8% gap from 52-week highs signal mean reversion risk; geopolitical tension (Iran strikes) and VIX uptick suggest caution despite bullish technicals. Expect modest positive open driven by cyclical strength, but watch for profit-taking at resistance.
morningBrief: OMX30 set for a tentative +0.3% to +0.5% open on the back of strong bull momentum (up 9.8% vs 200-MA, ADX 33.6 bullish) and commodity support from copper (+0.69%). Stochastic overbought (K=81.4, D=86.3) and RSI climbing toward 70 flag near-term consolidation or pullback risk—watch resistance at 3180 (50-MA) and upper Bollinger band. Industrials (Atlas Copco, Volvo, Sandvik) likely to outperform on copper strength and stable USD/SEK (9.303). Banks (SEB, Handelsbanken) neutral as yields decline, reducing NIM accretion. Key watch: Boliden on copper tailwind; H&M exposed to weak EUR/SEK (10.83). Geopolitical premium in VIX (16.77) may limit upside; US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET could shift risk tone.
drivers: OMX30 in confirmed bull market, +9.8% above 200-MA with strong ADX 33.6
Copper +0.69% supports industrials and mining; gold steady supports Boliden
US yields declining (-0.61%) reduces refinancing pressure; NASDAQ flat limits tech headwind
Geopolitical risk (Iran strikes) keeps VIX elevated at 16.77 but contained; defensive rotation possible

sectorImpacts.reason: Copper +0.69% and stable USD/SEK (9.303) support exporters; no major headwind from oil decline (-6.39%) as input cost relief offsets
Copper +0.69% and gold stable (+0.08%) directly boost revenue; Boliden is dual beneficiary of both commodity moves
US 10Y yields declined 0.61%, reducing NIM expansion benefit; no tailwind today despite bull momentum
Falling US yields (-0.61%) ease refinancing cost pressure; modest relief for property valuations
Weak EUR/SEK (10.83, -0.14%) increases sourcing costs for H&M; oil decline (-6.39%) neutral; geopolitical sentiment may dampen discretionary spending
NASDAQ flat (+0.19%) limits sentiment impact; falling yields reduce DCF headwind; no material tailwind
Geopolitical risk premium (VIX +1.08%) favors defensive rotation; AstraZeneca benefits from risk-off positioning

watchList.reason: Copper +0.69% and gold steady (+0.08%) are direct revenue drivers; stock well-positioned if commodity strength persists
Industrial cyclical with USD/SEK stable; benefits from copper-driven activity and export strength
Weak EUR/SEK (10.83) increases input cost headwinds; discretionary sentiment softened by geopolitical premium
Risk-off sentiment (VIX +1.08%, geopolitical) favors defensive pharma rotation; stable defensive anchor
Stochastic overbought (K=81.4) and RSI approaching 70 signal consolidation; watch 3180 (50-MA) and upper Bollinger band

econCalendarAlert: No major Swedish economic releases today. US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET (prior 229k) could shift risk sentiment; weak claims may support equities, strong claims (>240k) could trigger pullback given elevated VIX. Monitor Iran-US escalation headlines closely.
topRisk: Geopolitical escalation (Iran strikes, trade tensions) could spike VIX above 18 and trigger profit-taking from overbought technicals; stochastic already signaling mean reversion. Downside break of 3085 support would invalidate bull setup.
technicalSummary: MACD above signal with positive histogram (+10.434); strong ADX 33.6 with bullish DI bias
Overbought conditions: RSI 61.42 (approaching 70), Stochastic K=81.4, D=86.3—mean reversion risk imminent
Bollinger Band: %B=0.92 (upper half, elevated); width 6.09% shows volatility compression
ROC-90: +6.1% strong momentum, but 52-week position at 93% of range leaves limited upside runway
